name: Asset Compromise
desc: Signals intelligence indicates at least one person in the fort is transmitting operational data to an outside party. Contract details are being leaked before acceptance. Personnel morale is compromised.
flavour: The encrypted burst was logged in the comms archive three days ago. You found it. You are not surprised. You are assigning consequences.
flavourResolved: The asset was identified. They were given a choice between alternatives. They chose the sensible option.

name: Wet Contract on the Commander
desc: An open bounty has been placed on the commanding officer through an anonymous dark-market channel. The listed figure is offensively modest. Three contractors have already attempted collection. A fourth is expected.
flavour: The going market rate for your termination is less than a mid-range mana-rifle. You are taking this personally and professionally.
flavourResolved: The bounty has been revoked. The revocation was not voluntary on the part of whoever posted it.

name: Financial Infrastructure Embargo
desc: A nation has applied regulatory pressure on regional mana-credit networks to freeze Iron Company accounts and block transfer authorisation. Your revenue from operations in their territory has been cut off. This is a slow-acting economic strangulation and they know it.
flavour: Mana-credit is just weaponised logistics. They are using it correctly. So will you.
flavourResolved: The embargo has been circumvented. It cost them more operational goodwill than it cost you.

name: Accord Delegitimisation Campaign
desc: A nation is working through the Continental Accord's back-channel networks to strip the Iron Company of operational legitimacy. Client relationships are being warned off. Allied operators are being pressured to distance themselves through licensing threats.
flavour: The weapon they are deploying against you is made of regulatory paperwork. Regulatory paperwork still has teeth.
flavourResolved: The delegitimisation campaign has been countered through our own back-channel contacts. Our standing with Accord licensing is intact.

name: State-Funded Competitor
desc: A nation has capitalised and licensed a rival PMC with an explicit operational brief: undercut Iron Company contracts, discredit our reputation in the market, and ultimately absorb or destroy the company. They are well-funded and have been specifically head-hunting against our roster profile.
flavour: We have their personnel files. They were hired to fight us specifically. Someone briefed them well.
flavourResolved: The competitor has been dissolved. Their state sponsor is revising their containment strategy.

name: Arcane Compliance Audit
desc: The Pale Conclave's Enforcement Division has arrived with a valid warrant, portable resonance scanners, and specific questions about the fort's arcane operations. They have legal jurisdiction in two regional frameworks. This is not an informal visit.
flavour: The lead auditor has exceptionally clean hands and a very thorough warrant. You have noticed both.
flavourResolved: The auditors have concluded their visit. The warrant has been filed in the appropriate location — deep in the fort archive.

name: Combined Arms Demonstration
desc: A national military force has initiated 'scheduled training exercises' on the primary access corridor to the fort. Forty-five hundred personnel, mana-artillery units, and the barely-maintained fiction that the timing is unrelated to our recent operational activity.
flavour: They are not engaging. They are demonstrating capacity. You are taking the demonstration seriously.
flavourResolved: The exercise concluded ahead of schedule. We ensured they had operational reasons to wrap it early.

name: Authorised Suppression Campaign
desc: A full multi-unit suppression operation has been launched against the Iron Company. Combined-arms coordination, intelligence integration, and explicit orders to terminate the organisation at source. This is not a threat assessment. This is already an active operation.
flavour: We did not build this to be ended quietly by someone else's paperwork.
flavourResolved: The suppression campaign has been broken. They will need significant time to regroup and reauthorise. Use it.
